title: Laurentia irasuensis
taxonomy: Laurentia irasuensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is classified as a member of the genus Laurentia. The species was first described in 1993. Laurentia irasuensis is native to Mexico. Within the Boraginaceae family, it belongs to the order Lamiales.

intro: Laurentia irasuensis, also known as Laurentia, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Campanulaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments, such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Laurentia irasuensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Laurentia irasuensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to promote health and well-being.
